situational_context: Running comeback underway: 2 runs completed since gap broken on 2026-05-03 (after 182 days off). Run #1: 2.47 km (May 3), Run #2: 2.01 km (May 9). Primary modality remains e-bike commuting (~38 km/day round trip). Chronic load slowly building (22.3, up from 17.8 on April 19). ACWR at 0.75 — safely below ramp cap, in rebuild zone. HRV stable (weekly avg 39, BALANCED status); RHR improved to 49 bpm (was 53 in mid-April). VO2max running estimate revised down to 38.3 (post-running activity update). Weight declining steadily: 104.1 kg (vs 106.9 kg April 14). Sleep adequate at 7.2h 7d avg. No new labs since Dec 2025. Comeback protocol (return-to-running.md) active — tissue tolerance, not cardio, is the limiting factor. Vitamin D retest due ~July 2026 (8 weeks post-comeback).

recommendations.text: Continue running comeback at current pace: 1-2 short runs per week (≤3 km each), maintaining ≥48h between runs. Do not increase distance faster than 10% per session.
recommendations.measurable: Run #3 completed this week, ≤3 km, no post-run pain lasting >24h

recommendations.text: Begin eccentric calf raises 3×/week — primary injury-prevention measure given body weight and Achilles risk profile during running comeback
recommendations.measurable: ≥3 calf-raise sessions logged in the next 7 days

recommendations.text: Maintain Vitamin D supplementation daily; schedule blood panel for ~July 1, 2026 (8 weeks post-comeback) to confirm levels rising toward 40 ng/mL target

markers_to_watch.threshold: Alert if RHR rises >5 bpm above 49 bpm baseline for 3+ consecutive days
markers_to_watch.reason: RHR has improved to 49 bpm; elevation would indicate overload or sub-clinical illness during comeback

markers_to_watch.threshold: Target gradual increase toward 35–40 over next 4 weeks
markers_to_watch.reason: Currently at 1.4% of historical peak — structured running will progressively raise this

markers_to_watch.threshold: Any localized joint/tendon pain lasting >24h after a run triggers skip of next run
markers_to_watch.reason: Tissue tolerance is the bottleneck at 104 kg body weight; pain signals early overload
